LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF ANA CUEVAS' UNOPPOSED MOTION TO DISMISS ENTIRE PAGA ACTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE IS CONTINUED FOR HEARING TO APRIL 9, 2026. PLAINTIFF'S SUBMISSION GIVES THE COURT INSUFFICIENT INFORMATION FROM WHICH TO DETERMINE THAT THE SETTLEMENT OF THIS PAGA ACTION, WHICH THE COURT IS REQUIRED TO APPROVE AS TO THE DISPOSITION OF THE PAGA CLAIMS, IS FAIR TO ABSENT AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES. BY ONE WEEK IN ADVANCE OF THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE, PLAINTIFF MUST FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF, WITH SUPPORTING DECLARATIONS IF NEEDED, THAT ALLOWS THE COURT EITHER TO CONCLUDE THAT THE AMOUNT OF PLAINTIFF'S SETTLEMENT IS REASONABLE AND DOES NOT LEVERAGE THE DISMISSED CLAIMS OF ABSENT AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES TO PLAINTIFF'S BENEFIT, OR THAT OTHER FACTORS SUPPLY GOOD CAUSE TO APPROVE THE DISMISSAL AS FAIR TO ABSENT EMPLOYEES. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

Off Calendar Apr-22-2026 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Amended Motion To Dismiss Entire Paga Action Without Prejudice; Declaration Of Joseph C. Ramli; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Apr-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED MOTION TO DISMISS ENTIRE PAGA ACTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion to dismiss the entire PAGA action without prejudice is granted. The court finds that on the facts presented here, and for good cause shown in the moving papers, the settlement of Plaintiff's individual claim and the dismissal without prejudice of all claims is fair and reasonable in view of PAGA's purposes. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-19-2026 Continued To May-19-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Amended Motion To Dismiss Entire Paga Action Without Prejudice; Declaration Of Joseph C. Ramli; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Mar-09-2026 Continued To Apr-09-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference

Joseph C. Ramli, Esq., JUSTICE FOR WORKERS, P.C. 323-922-2000 jramli@justiceforworkers.com
Attorney for Plaintiff ANA MARIA CUEVAS

Sat Sang Khalsa, Esq. GORDON REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP skhalsa@grsm.com Attorney for Defendant International Training & Exchange, Inc. d/b/a Aupaircare, Inc.

Hearing held. Discussed, not ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANA CUEVAS , ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AS AN AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEE, THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, AND OTHER AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES Notice Of Motion And Amended Motion To Dismiss Entire Paga Action Without Prejudice

The unopposed motion to dismiss entire PAGA action without prejudice is continued for hearing to April 9, 2026. Plaintiff's submission gives the court insufficient information from which to determine that the settlement of this PAGA action, which the court is required to approve as to the disposition of the PAGA claims, is fair to absent aggrieved employees. By one week in advance of the continued hearing date, Plaintiff must file a supplemental brief, with supporting declarations if needed, that allows the court either to conclude that the amount of Plaintiff's settlement is reasonable and does not leverage the dismissed claims of absent aggrieved employees to Plaintiff's benefit, or that other factors supply good cause to approve the dismissal as fair to absent employees.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Entire Paga Action Without Prejudice; Declaration Of Joseph C. Ramli; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANA CUEVAS, THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, AND OTHER AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES' Motion To Dismiss Entire PAGA Action Without Prejudice.

The unopposed motion to dismiss is denied without prejudice. An individual settlement that dismisses PAGA claims without prejudice is a PAGA settlement that must be approved by the court. The moving papers provide no information from which the court can determine whether the abandonment of representative claims is fair to aggrieved absent employees, who may have foregone filing claims in light of the filing of this case.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
